data.four_set_effect: When the character equipping this artifact set heals a character in the party, a Sea-Dyed Foam will appear for 3 seconds, accumulating the amount of HP recovered from healing (including overflow healing). At the end of the duration, the Sea-Dyed Foam will explode, dealing DMG to nearby opponents based on 90% of the accumulated healing. (This DMG is calculated similarly to Reactions such as Electro-Charged, and Superconduct, but is not affected by Elemental Mastery, Character Levels, or Reaction DMG Bonuses). Only one Sea-Dyed Foam can be produced every 3.5 seconds. Each Sea-Dyed Foam can accumulate up to 30,000 HP (including overflow healing). There can be no more than one Sea-Dyed Foam active at any given time. This effect can still be triggered even when the character who is using this artifact set is not on the field.

circlet_of_logos.desc: An ancient, intricate crown that was once used by a forgotten clergy member. Today, this relic has been enshrined with great ceremony by the people of Watatsumi.
circlet_of_logos.title: Crown of Watatsumi

flower_of_life.desc: A soft flower that has taken on the many shades of the capricious ocean. It shines with wondrous colors under the moon's silver light.
flower_of_life.title: Sea-Dyed Blossom

goblet_of_eonothem.desc: The shining pearls that the shrine maidens of Watatsumi Island offer up shine eternally and never dim.
goblet_of_eonothem.title: Pearl Cage

plume_of_death.desc: A down feather with the same hue as coral, said to come from a shrine maiden's ceremonial garment.
plume_of_death.title: Deep Palace's Plume

sands_of_eon.desc: A clean, flawless seashell that comes from the bottomless ocean.
sands_of_eon.title: Cowry of Parting

list.desc: A tender flower from the ocean. Its core is adorned with pure pearls.</br>The songs of Watatsumi Island say that these flowers bloom in the pearl-lit depths.</br>Steeped in the lovesickness of the diving sea-daughters and the gentle moonlight, they would give off a pearly gleam.</br>When all conflicts have ceased, the sea beasts shall no longer weep for their lonesome companions,</br>And when the moonlight rises over Touzan, the lovely deity shall rise and sing.</br>"Come, sea-daughters, come and see, O people of my heart, for the moon is out tonight."</br>"Though Touzan might fall this night, the lightning and the storm can never hide the moon's pearly glory..."</br>The lonesome maiden will sing and dance upon the moon-silver waves.</br>The divers shall forget the pain of loss, and even the tender flowers will regain their vibrance.</br>
list.title: Sea-Dyed Blossom

list.desc: Omikami once selected his clergy from amongst the people of Watatsumi, personally giving them these circlets of office.</br>But when the age of the martyrdom passed, and the old priesthood passed with it, these lovely crowns were sealed.</br>The people of the sea sing that these crowns of pearls and coral will never be stained by any dirt,</br>And that any who should have the fortune to receive one were recognized as rulers by Omikami.</br>The valiant vassal lord known as Touzannou and the twins who traversed the waves freely...</br>They all had Omikami's tender gaze and favor, and have been immortalized in the islanders' songs.</br>It is said that these rulers once served as the deity's auxiliaries, guiding the people of the sea to farm, fish, and hunt on their island.</br>But as the time of destined martyrdom came, a god would fall.</br>Bearing the hopes and memories that were brought up from the ocean depths, and steeped in a history and culture that are now lost,</br>These intricate and enchanting crowns passed with their owners into the forgotten rifts.</br>
list.title: Crown of Watatsumi

list.desc: To the sea people, the bright pearls that Watatsumi's deity once praised are priceless treasures.</br>Only the Divine Priestess had the right to sing the great song themed around those pearls.</br>Legend has it that the rainbow-hued clams produced these flawless gems in gratitude for Watatsumi's tenderness.</br>In latter days, it was even said that the bloodline of the Divine Priestess was originally birthed from these pearls.</br>Having stepped from her soft, vivid cradle, she and the sisters who danced with the sea and moon were greatly favored,</br>For out of his rejoicing and love, Omikami gave unto them beautiful gems that granted them the pure desire to pursue daylight.</br>In the hands of those whose veins run true with Watatsumi blood, pearls seem to gleam brighter than normal.</br>Perhaps this is but an ancient tale, the truth of which can no longer be verified.</br>But it is said that at the point of defeat, the Divine Priestess and the twin sisters exchanged their clothes and concealed themselves within the endless waves.</br>Only this bright pearl was lost in the rippling tumult, returning to the silent solitude of the depths.</br>
list.title: Pearl Cage

list.desc: In the days when the many clans first saw the light of day, Omikami chose shrine maidens from among the people of the sea.</br>In the island-songs of history, the first Divine Priestess was once one of the sea-daughters who collected pearls.</br>She would come amongst the children, who in their meaningless conflict, lost sight of their future,</br>And amongst the old who had lost sight of life's beauty amidst ruthless tragedy.</br>The Divine Priestess comforted the people using her lovely songs and gentle words,</br>And even amidst the stormy times, the people of Watatsumi saw hope for the first time.</br>This sea-born feather was said to have come from that Divine Priestess' ceremonial garb.</br>It was plucked by mistake by a child's tender hand, and was preserved by a fretful person.</br>Later, when the brave hero and priestesses rushed to their irrevocable sacrifice,</br>The Divine Priestess' ceremonial garment was not lost, but passed down in memory to the present day.</br>
list.title: Deep Palace's Plume

list.desc: Time seems to pass exceedingly slowly in the silent, fluorescent ocean depths.</br>Even the bright seashells might grow forgetful as the years lengthen.</br>The people of Watatsumi once came forth from the darkness, and thus bade farewell to the long dreams of the deep.</br>They escaped the prying gaze of the Dragonheirs in the dark, walking the glimmering coral stair into the realm of daylight.</br>It is said that at that time, the ocean people would take a single seashell as remembrance for their clan.</br>And as for those who had lost theirs, they would be welcomed into a new family.</br>In the ancient tongue, these pure shells would become known as the Parcels of Reunion.</br>Two parties who embrace shall not again be parted by an outside force, though this enjoining is not itself eternal.</br>These seashells, therefore, were a farewell to life beneath the waves, and the beginning of a new life under the sun.</br>
list.title: Cowry of Parting
